column4: Confirm that all vector CSR properties are properly set with regards to read write, clear, and set privileges with the csr instructions
column5: With each of the vector CSRs conduct a read, write, clear, and set operation on the CSR
column6: Will abide by spec's read and write permissions for each csr
column12: The vector extension adds 32 vector registers, and seven unprivileged CSRs (vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, vtype, vl, vlenb) to a base scalar RISC-V ISA.
column14: The XLEN-bit-wide read-only vl CSR can only be updated by the vset{i}vl{i} instructions, and the fault-only-first vector load instruction variants.
column15: read-only XLEN-wide vector type CSR, vtype provides the default type used to interpret the contents of the vector register file, and can only be updated by vset{i}vl{i}

column12: vstart, vxrm, vxsat, vcsr are all writable

column4: Confirm that all combinations of lmul crossed with sew correctly set the vtype register in accordance with the vill field
column5: Use all vset{i}vli instructions to cross eSEW={8/16/32/64} with mLMUL = {f8/f4/f2/1/2/4/8} rd and uimm can be any nonzero value
column6: vtype.vill becomes 1 and other bits become 0 on illegal combinations, such as sew = 1xx.
column12: Implementations must consider all bits of the vtype value to determine if the configuration is supported. An unsupported value in any location within the vtype value must result in vill being set. In particular, all XLEN bits of the register vtype argument to the vsetvl instruction must be checked. Implementations cannot ignore fields they do not implement. All bits must be checked to ensure that new code assuming unsupported vector features in vtype traps instead of executing incorrectly on an older implementation....The supported element width may vary with LMUL
column13: The use of vtype encodings with LMUL < SEWMIN/ELEN is reserved, but implementations can set vill if they do not support these configurations.
